key_insight: GAIA learns PDE operators on arbitrary geometries by turning boundary and interior information into geometry tokens that condition adaptive integral layers, enabling one architecture for forward, boundary value, and inverse problems.
engineering_relevance: This can replace separate solvers or iterative inversion pipelines with a single amortized model for fast simulation and reconstruction on complex domains.
method: Encodes domain boundary and interior fields into geometry tokens.
Uses cross-attention to condition integral transform kernels on local geometric features.
Runs forward and inverse tasks in one pass without retraining, graph construction, or iterative optimization.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on seven 2D/3D benchmarks, including four new or extended inverse/BVP tasks, and reported new state-of-the-art results on every inverse and BVP setting plus strong stability across point resolutions.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the geometry-token representation, so generalization to highly out-of-distribution geometries or meshes may still require careful validation.
paper_title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
